findings.detail: Pipeline steps are non-focusable <div> elements activated only via click delegation. Keyboard users cannot move focus to a step or activate it with Enter/Space.
findings.fix: Use <button type="button"> per step (or role="button" tabindex="0" with keydown for Enter/Space), preserve visible labels, and wire the same handler.

findings.detail: Project list entries are <div class="dropdown-item"> with click handlers only; they are not in the tab order and have no keyboard activation.
findings.fix: Use <button type="button" class="dropdown-item"> or make each item focusable with tabindex="0" and handle Enter/Space; consider aria-activedescendant pattern on the combobox-style control.

findings.detail: Several inputs and selects lack an associated <label for="..."> (or aria-labelledby). Placeholder alone does not meet programmatic name / label requirements.
findings.fix: Add visible <label for="id"> (or aria-label where a visible label is redundant) for each control; ensure i18n covers label text.

findings.detail: Dynamically generated <label> elements are not associated with inputs: labels do not use for="" matching input id, and labels do not wrap controls.
findings.fix: Add for attributes on each label matching the corresponding input/textarea/select id.

findings.detail: Provider and model labels are not programmatically tied to the controls (no for/id linkage).

New Project modal sets initial focus on the name field but does not trap focus. Tab/Shift+Tab can move focus to interactive elements behind the overlay, unlike #crud-modal and #confirm-dialog which use _trapFocus.
findings.fix: Reuse the same focus trap pattern as CRUD/confirm modals on the create overlay; restore focus to the triggering control on close.

findings.detail: Returning to the project list is bound to click on the logo only; the heading is not keyboard-focusable, so keyboard users lack an equivalent to deselect the project.
findings.fix: Expose a dedicated focusable control (e.g. button with accessible name) or tabindex="0" and keyboard activation for the same action.

findings.detail: Header checkbox for select-all has no accessible name in the table header context.
findings.fix: Add aria-label or an associated <label> (visually hidden if needed), e.g. "Select all test cases".

findings.detail: Container uses role="status" and aria-live="polite"; individual toasts are plain divs. Error messages are not distinguished with role="alert" or assertive live regions, so urgent feedback may be delayed behind other polite announcements.
findings.fix: For type === "error", set role="alert" (or aria-live="assertive") on the toast node; keep polite for success/info if desired.

findings.detail: Toast text is white (#fff) on #00b894 (success) and #e17055 (error). Contrast ratios are approximately 2.5:1 and 3.2:1, below 4.5:1 for normal text.
findings.fix: Darken background colors, use darker text on light toast backgrounds, or increase font size/weight to qualify as large text (3:1) if appropriate.

notes: Contrast checks used CSS variables from style.css; toast ratios computed for #ffffff on #00b894 and #e17055. Tab order in static HTML is generally left-to-right; hidden tab panels use display:none. Detail panel focuses #detail-close on open; no full trap. CRUD and confirm modals implement focus cycling via _trapFocus.
